approach: Delete the `init` and `mode` capability from the cyberfleet CLI entirely (#225). The `.agents/cyberfleet/ship.json` marker held only `{version:1}`, its sole reader was `detectMode`, and `detectMode`'s sole caller was the `cyberfleet mode` command itself — a closed loop gating no capability. `missions`/`jump`/`pause`/`gate` never consulted it; no hook or statusline did either.

Delete the `@frozen` `init/` (4 scenarios) and `mode/` (4 scenarios) behavioral nodes from this spec. Fires the Clearance hard floor: 8 acceptance scenarios removed from a `status: implemented` spec whose spec+impl gates were both ratified by unional personally (cr `cyberfleet-mode-init`).

Delete `packages/cyberfleet/src/{mode,init}.ts` + their tests + the two CLI verbs. Fleet membership already lives in `cyberlegion unit register` -> AgentRecord, which is what `missions` enumerates (joins ship->CR on `worktree.branch`); the marker was a shadow registry with no readers.

This spec becomes descriptive-only (no behavioral nodes remain) and its charter sharpens: cyberfleet = the SDD-derived mission view + gates; cyberlegion = the sessions. `init` is deleted, not stubbed — re-add when a ship genuinely needs on-disk config.
why: leash auto-none: deletes 8 acceptance scenarios across two @frozen suites on an implemented spec, which fires the Clearance hard floor (not self-assertable). The owner (unional) holds the user channel live this session, found the defect, and directed the deletion, so every gate verdict here is human-ratified rather than agent-self-asserted. Companion shard to `.agents/specs/cyberfleet-plugin/ledger/cyberfleet-mode-pod-precondition.440ca1.jsonl` — one CR, two touched specs.
